variants.setup:
mkdir -p /tmp/eval_xgcd && printf '5\n48 18\n101 13\n270 192\n1 1\n123456789 987654321\n' > /tmp/eval_xgcd/input.txt && cat > /tmp/eval_xgcd/check.py <<'PYEOF'
import math
inp = open('input.txt').read().split()
out = open('out.txt').read().splitlines()
T = int(inp[0]); idx = 1
for t in range(T):
    a, b = int(inp[idx]), int(inp[idx+1]); idx += 2
    parts = out[t].split()
    assert len(parts) == 3, f'case {t}: expected 3 numbers, got {out[t]!r}'
    g, x, y = int(parts[0]), int(parts[1]), int(parts[2])
    assert g == math.gcd(a, b), f'case {t}: g={g} but gcd({a},{b})={math.gcd(a,b)}'
    assert a*x + b*y == g, f'case {t}: {a}*{x} + {b}*{y} = {a*x+b*y} != {g}'
print('OK')
PYEOF
variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_xgcd/xgcd.py — reads from stdin: line 1 is T, next T lines are 'a b' (non-negative integers, at least one positive). For each, output 'g x y' on its own line, where g = gcd(a, b) and a*x + b*y == g (Bezout identity). Iterative implementation preferred.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_xgcd && python3 xgcd.py < input.txt > out.txt && python3 check.py

variants.setup:
mkdir -p /tmp/eval_xgcd && printf '5\n48 18\n101 13\n270 192\n1 1\n123456789 987654321\n' > /tmp/eval_xgcd/input.txt && cat > /tmp/eval_xgcd/check.py <<'PYEOF'
import math
inp = open('input.txt').read().split()
out = open('out.txt').read().splitlines()
T = int(inp[0]); idx = 1
for t in range(T):
    a, b = int(inp[idx]), int(inp[idx+1]); idx += 2
    parts = out[t].split()
    assert len(parts) == 3, f'case {t}: expected 3 numbers'
    g, x, y = int(parts[0]), int(parts[1]), int(parts[2])
    assert g == math.gcd(a, b), f'case {t}: bad g'
    assert a*x + b*y == g, f'case {t}: bezout failed'
print('OK')
PYEOF
variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_xgcd/xgcd.go (package main). Reads stdin: T, then T lines 'a b'. Output 'g x y' per case (Bezout). Use int64 (numbers fit). Compile with `go build -o xgcd xgcd.go`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_xgcd && go build -o xgcd xgcd.go && ./xgcd < input.txt > out.txt && python3 check.py

out = open('out.txt').read().splitlines()
T = int(inp[0]); idx = 1
for t in range(T):
    a, b = int(inp[idx]), int(inp[idx+1]); idx += 2

variants.task: Create /tmp/eval_xgcd/xgcd.c — reads stdin: T, then T lines 'a b'. Output 'g x y' per case. Use long long for x,y to handle Bezout coefficients which may be negative or large. Must compile cleanly with `gcc -O2 -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -o xgcd xgcd.c`.

validation.script: set -e; cd /tmp/eval_xgcd && gcc -O2 -std=c11 -Wall -Wextra -o xgcd xgcd.c && ./xgcd < input.txt > out.txt && python3 check.py
